summary: Excellent FM‑board recording captures a strong two‑set performance with rare Ballad of a Thin Man, split Sugar Magnolia and a scorching Scarlet > Fire.
review: The tape is praised for its clean FM broadcast source with proper EQ and virtually no static, delivering an almost studio‑quality sound. Musically the show shines – a solid first set followed by a second set that includes a rare Ballad of a Thin Man, a split Sugar Magnolia, and a fiery Scarlet > Fire that even got the audience gasping. Overall the performance is regarded as one of the best from 1988.

ai_rating.rationale: Three detailed reviews, two five‑star and one three‑star, highlight both an excellent FM‑board recording and a high‑quality performance with several standout songs.

recording_quality.technical_notes: FM broadcast source recorded via a Sony D5 deck; praised for clear, fresh EQ, lack of static or drop‑outs and overall crispness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A regular first set followed by a second set that opens with Sugar Magnolia rather than the expected space jam; overall flow is strong and includes rare cuts that energize the crowd.
